title: Portuguese Pork Skewers
description: A flavorful and easy-to-make dish from Portugal, perfect for a summer dinner.

ingredients.name: Pork shoulder, cut into 1-inch cubes
ingredients.notes: You can ask your butcher to cut the pork for you.

ingredients.name: Portuguese red wine (or substitute with other red wine)
ingredients.notes: This adds depth to the sauce.

ingredients.name: Extra virgin olive oil
ingredients.notes: Use a high-quality oil for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Garlic, minced
ingredients.notes: You can adjust the amount to your taste.

ingredients.name: Fresh oregano, chopped
ingredients.notes: This adds a nice herbal flavor.

ingredients.name: Salt and pepper
ingredients.notes: Use freshly ground black pepper for the best flavor.

ingredients.name: Crispy breadcrumbs
ingredients.notes: You can make your own breadcrumbs by blending bread in a food processor.

instructions: Preheat your grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
In a large bowl, whisk together red wine, olive oil, garlic, oregano, salt, and pepper.
Add the pork cubes to the bowl and mix until they're well coated with the marinade.
Thread the pork cubes onto skewers, leaving a small space between each piece.
Season the pork with salt and pepper to taste.
Grill the skewers for 8-10 minutes per side, or until the pork is cooked through.
While the pork is cooking, mix the crispy breadcrumbs with a pinch of salt.
Once the pork is done, brush the skewers with the remaining marinade and sprinkle with breadcrumbs.
Serve the skewers hot, garnished with fresh oregano if desired.
